perturbation.original_text: The Joint Venture is formed for the purpose of engaging generally in the business of marketing batteries and related products, (the "Products") that include the display of licensed logos, images, brand names and other labels that differentiate them from the branding (the "PVSS Products") under which PVSS and/or its affiliates, sell to retailers and distributors in the normal course of their business.
perturbation.changed_text: The Joint Venture is formed for the purpose of engaging generally in the business of marketing batteries and related products, (the "Items") that include the display of licensed logos, images, brand names and other labels that differentiate them from the branding (the "PVSS Products") under which PVSS and/or its affiliates, sell to retailers and distributors in the normal course of their business.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Products' to 'Items' creates confusion about whether the Joint Venture is solely focused on batteries ('Products') or if it includes other unspecified 'Items.' This contradicts the specificity implied by the definition of 'Products' elsewhere and in the exhibit, leading to uncertainty in the venture's scope.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Scope of the Joint Venture Business. The Joint Venture is formed for the purpose of engaging generally in the business of marketing batteries and related products, (the "Items") that include the display of licensed logos, images, brand names and other labels that differentiate them from the branding (the "PVSS Products") under which PVSS and/or its affiliates, sell to retailers and distributors in the normal course of their business. Without in any way limiting the generality of the foregoing, the business of the Joint Venture shall include: (a) The purchase of Products for resale; (b) The acquisition of a license(s) permitting the use of selected images in the Products; (c) The sale and distribution of the Products to retailers and distributors; and, (d) The transaction of such other and further business as is necessary, advisable, or incidental to the business of the Joint Venture. (e) Develop a global marketing program for licensed Products Exhibit A attached hereto, describes by way of example but not limitation the responsibilities of the Joint Venturers
perturbation.justification: The original text defines the scope as 'marketing batteries and related products, (the "Products")'. The changed text broadens this to 'Items', but the contradicted text later specifies 'the purchase of Products' and refers to 'Exhibit A' describing 'Products'. The change from 'Products' to 'Items' introduces an ambiguity, as now the venture could be dealing with more than just batteries and related products, but the later sections still refer specifically to 'Products' and related details found in the Exhibit. This contradiction is a misalignment of terminology because while the first section is using 'Items', the rest of the document is using 'Products', without providing a clear connection between the two. This makes it unclear what the scope and responsibilities actually are.

perturbation.original_text: The Joint Venturers shall have full and complete charge of all affairs of the Joint Venture. The Joint Venturers recognize that both of the Joint Venturers are and will continue to be engaged in the conduct of their respective businesses for their own account. Neither Joint Venturer shall be entitled to compensation for services rendered to the Joint Venture as such, but each Joint Venturer shall be reimbursed for all direct expenses, including travel, office, and all other out-of-pocket expenses incurred in the operation of the affairs of the Joint Venture and the promotion of its businesses.
perturbation.changed_text: The Parties shall have full and complete charge of all affairs of the Joint Venture. The Parties recognize that both of the Joint Venturers are and will continue to be engaged in the conduct of their respective businesses for their own account. Neither Joint Venturer shall be entitled to compensation for services rendered to the Joint Venture as such, but each Joint Venturer shall be reimbursed for all direct expenses, including travel, office, and all other out-of-pocket expenses incurred in the operation of the affairs of the Joint Venture and the promotion of its businesses.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Joint Venturers' to 'Parties' at the beginning of this section implies that anyone considered a 'Party' (which can also refer to singular entities) has full charge, instead of both Joint Venturers acting together. It creates an in-text contradiction between 'Parties' having full charge and the later statement that 'each Joint Venturer' requires consent from 'both' to take certain actions.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Powers and Authority of the Joint Venturers. The Parties shall have full and complete charge of all affairs of the Joint Venture. The Joint Venturers recognize that both of the Joint Venturers are and will continue to be engaged in the conduct of their respective businesses for their own account. Neither Joint Venturer shall be entitled to compensation for services rendered to the Joint Venture as such, but each Joint Venturer shall be reimbursed for all direct expenses, including travel, office, and all other out-of-pocket expenses incurred in the operation of the affairs of the Joint Venture and the promotion of its businesses. It is agreed that either Joint Venturer shall, except as provided for below, have authority to execute instruments of any character relating to the affairs of the Joint Venture; provided, that without the written consent or approval of both of the Joint Venturers: (i) the Joint Venture shall incur no liability of any sort, nor any indebtedness for borrowed funds; (ii) no assets owned in the name of the Joint Venture be disposed of; and (iii) no commitment to purchase any item for the Joint Venture shall be made. 39
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Joint Venturers' with 'Parties' at the beginning of the section within the 'Powers and Authority of the Joint Venturers' section itself. The explanation indicates this creates a contradiction because 'Parties' can refer to a singular entity, while the contradicted section emphasizes the requirement for 'both of the Joint Venturers' consent for certain actions. This introduces ambiguity and a direct contradiction on who has the power and authority since 'parties' could imply just one party has full charge, while the original intent required both Joint Venturers' consent.

perturbation.original_text: All direct costs and expenses, which shall include any insurance costs in connection with the distribution of the Products or operations of the Joint Venture, or if the business of the Joint Venture requires additional office facilities than those now presently maintained by each Joint Venturer, such item shall be paid by the Joint Venture.
perturbation.changed_text: All direct costs and expenses, which shall include any insurance costs in connection with the distribution of the Products or operations of the Joint Venture, or if the business of the Joint Venture requires additional office facilities than those now presently maintained by each Partner, such item shall be paid by the Joint Venture.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Joint Venturer' to 'Partner' introduces a contradiction because the agreement explicitly states in the preamble that the entities are business associates and not partners. This creates uncertainty because it contradicts the initial declaration, raising doubts about the legal relationship and financial responsibilities.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Internal Revenue Code Election. The Joint Venturers agree and declare that this association for the carrying on of a joint venture business operation does not, and is not intended to create a partnership, for either legal or United States income tax purposes, each Party recognizing that the other is willing and able to contribute capital, labor, and services for the operation of a successful joint venture business. Further, each Party elects under the authority of Section 761(a) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 (the "Code"), as amended and all successor statutes, to be excluded from the application of all of the provisions of Subchapter K of Chapter 1 of the Subtitle A of the Code, and the Parties agree that the election out of Subchapter K of Chapter 1 of Subtitle A of the Code shall, if necessary, be manifested by their execution and filing of all appropriate documentation. The Parties also declare that they are not making any agreement to undertake any business other than that set forth in this Agreement; and nothing in this Agreement is to be construed as a limitation of the powers or rights of either Party to carry on his separate business for his sole benefit; provided, however, the Parties shall cooperate with each other according to the terms and spirit of this Agreement in the performance of their joint venture business operation.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Joint Venturer' to 'Partner' directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text' which explicitly states that the agreement is not intended to create a partnership for any legal or United States income tax purposes. The altered text introduces terminology that creates a direct and strong conflict with this explicitly stated intention, creating a strong in-text contradiction.
